name: Sanatana Gosvami
dates_notes: Mughal court records (former finance official); dates well-established.

dates_evidence.description: Mughal-period court records of his pre-conversion administrative role.
Manuscript colophons across his Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta and Hari-Bhakti-Vilāsa.

core_thesis:
Sanātana Gosvāmī's contribution is to work the doctrine of *acintya-bhedābheda* into two complementary registers — narrative-theological and ritual-practical — through which the Gauḍīya tradition acquires liveable institutional and contemplative form. The shared doctrinal substrate is the school's settled position: Kṛṣṇa is *svayaṃ-bhagavān* (the school-defining verse, *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.3.28, glossed in *Vaiṣṇava-Toṣaṇī* ad loc. and reformulated in [*Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta* 2.1.69–70](cite://sanatana-gosvami/brhad-bhagavatamrta/2.1.69-70)); the *jīva* is eternal *taṭastha-śakti*, simultaneously different and non-different from him by *acintya-śakti* ([*Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta* 2.2.158–177](cite://sanatana-gosvami/brhad-bhagavatamrta/2.2.158-177), Gopa-Kumāra's instruction in Vaikuṇṭha); *bhakti* is the only *abhidheya* (means) and *prema* the only *prayojana* (end) ([*Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta* 1.7.122–129](cite://sanatana-gosvami/brhad-bhagavatamrta/1.7.122-129), Nārada's ascending hierarchy of devotees).

The *Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta* operates in the narrative register. Two extended quests dramatize the school's hierarchy: the *Pūrva-Khaṇḍa* follows Nārada through ascending classes of devotees to demonstrate that *prema-bhakti* in *mādhurya-rasa* — exemplified by the *gopīs* of Vraja — is the supreme form ([*Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta* 1.7](cite://sanatana-gosvami/brhad-bhagavatamrta/1.7)); the *Uttara-Khaṇḍa* follows Gopa-Kumāra through the cosmography of devotional realms to establish Goloka-Vṛndāvana as the highest attainment ([*Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta* 2.6–7](cite://sanatana-gosvami/brhad-bhagavatamrta/2.6-7)). The narrative form is doctrinally consequential because it embeds the technical *acintya-bhedābheda* claim — that the eschatological *jīva* enters real, eternal, individuated relationship with Bhagavān — in a form a reader can inhabit.

The *Hari-Bhakti-Vilāsa* operates in the ritual register. Commissioned by Caitanya and composed with Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī's collaboration on the practical-ritual material, it supplies the comprehensive *vaiṣṇava-smṛti* in twenty *vilāsa*-s — *dīkṣā* (*vilāsa* 1–2), *arcana* (*vilāsa* 5–9), *vaidhī-sādhana*, the *Ekādaśī* and Vaiṣṇava calendar (*vilāsa* 12–16), conduct and *prāyaścitta* — by which a *vaiṣṇava* community acquires durable form. Without this code the doctrinal vision could not have institutionalised; with it, the tradition could organize households, temples, and lineages in stable form.

The two registers are complementary, not redundant. The narrative theology establishes what is to be reached. The ritual code establishes how the practitioner is to live in the meantime. Sanātana is therefore the school's narrative theologian and its ritual codifier in one figure, and the institutional shape of Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇavism after his time is largely his work.

engaged_works.title: Brhad-Bhagavatamrta
engaged_works.summary: The *Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta* is Sanātana's two-part narrative-theological work. The *Pūrva-Khaṇḍa* (*Bhakta-Tattva*) follows the sage Nārada through a graded hierarchy of devotees ([*Bṛhad-Bhāgavatāmṛta* 1.1–7](cite://sanatana-gosvami/brhad-bhagavatamrta/1.1-7)), culminating in the *gopīs* of Vraja (1.7.122–129), to demonstrate by ascending example that *prema-bhakti* in *mādhurya-rasa* is the supreme form. The *Uttara-Khaṇḍa* (*Goloka-Tattva*) follows the brāhmaṇa Gopa-Kumāra through a graded cosmography of devotional realms — Svarga (2.1), the *Brahmajyoti* (2.2.157–200), Vaikuṇṭha (2.3), Ayodhyā (2.4), Dvārakā (2.5), and finally Goloka-Vṛndāvana (2.6–7) — to establish Kṛṣṇa-*līlā* in Goloka as the highest-ranked attainment. The narrative form is doctrinally consequential: it embeds the technical *acintya-bhedābheda* claim that the eschatological *jīva* enters real, eternal, individuated relationship with Bhagavān (the *brahmajyoti*-rejection at 2.2.158–177 fixes the school's anti-monistic eschatology), and never dissolves into impersonal *brahmajyoti*. The work carries Sanātana's auto-commentary, the *Dig-Darśinī*, which makes it function simultaneously as theological *kāvya* and as systematic exposition of Gauḍīya soteriology. The *Dig-Darśinī*'s presence is structurally important: it ensures that the doctrinal claims a reader might miss in the narrative are surfaced explicitly in the gloss.

engaged_works.title: Hari-Bhakti-Vilasa
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored Gauḍīya manual of practice.
engaged_works.summary: The *Hari-Bhakti-Vilāsa* is the Gauḍīya school's comprehensive *vaiṣṇava-smṛti*. Twenty *vilāsas* organize it. Sanātana composed it on Caitanya's commission, with Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī's collaboration on the practical-ritual material. The work codifies the entire ritual life of a Gauḍīya practitioner: *dīkṣā* (initiation) and the *guru-paramparā* (*Hari-Bhakti-Vilāsa* *vilāsa* 1–2); the structure of *mantra* (*vilāsa* 3–4); *arcana* of the *śāli-grāma* and the Deity (*vilāsa* 5–9); daily, periodic, and seasonal observances (*nitya*, *naimittika*; *vilāsa* 10–11); the Vaiṣṇava calendar with its *vratas* and *utsavas*, especially the *Ekādaśī* observances (*vilāsa* 12–16); rules of personal conduct, food, and association (*vilāsa* 17–19); the *prāyaścitta* (atonement) procedures (*vilāsa* 20). Doctrinally the work assumes *acintya-bhedābheda* and treats *vaidhī-bhakti* — rule-governed practice — as the structural scaffolding within which *rāgānugā-bhakti* matures (cf. Rūpa Gosvāmī, [*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 1.2.5–6](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/1.2.5-6) for the technical distinction). The relation between the two modes is deliberate: *vaidhī* is not provisional in the sense of being later canceled, but it is preparatory in the sense of forming the conditions under which spontaneous love can ripen. As the school's authoritative ritual code, the *Hari-Bhakti-Vilāsa* gave the Gauḍīya tradition the institutional form by which it survived and spread beyond Bengal.

engaged_works.title: Vaishnava-Toshani
engaged_works.summary: The *Vaiṣṇava-Toṣaṇī* is Sanātana's commentary on the tenth book of the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa*. The tenth book is the central textual locus for Kṛṣṇa-*līlā* and therefore the most consequential single passage of scripture for the school. Sanātana reads the book as the *Bhāgavata*'s self-disclosed climax, with Vraja-*līlā* as its still center. The school's doctrinal commitments are drawn out at the school-defining verses: *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.3.28 (*kṛṣṇas tu bhagavān svayam*) — though strictly part of book 1 — is invoked at the opening of book 10 (*Vaiṣṇava-Toṣaṇī* on *Bhāgavata* 10.1) for Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*; the *Rāsa-pañcādhyāyī* (*Vaiṣṇava-Toṣaṇī* on *Bhāgavata* 10.29–33) for the *gopīs*' *prema* as the paradigm of *mādhurya-rasa*; the structure of Vraja (*Vaiṣṇava-Toṣaṇī* on *Bhāgavata* 10.13–14, the *brahma-vimohana-līlā*) for the *nitya-siddha* status of its inhabitants; the conditions of *līlā*-participation (*Vaiṣṇava-Toṣaṇī* on *Bhāgavata* 10.47, the *bhramara-gīta*). Jīva Gosvāmī later abridges and supplements the work as the *Laghu-Vaiṣṇava-Toṣaṇī*, which becomes the more widely circulated form, but the substantive interpretive decisions are Sanātana's. The commentary is both the school's first sustained tenth-book exegesis and the textual basis on which the Gauḍīya theology of Vraja is built.
